title: Skip systemtest/050-signing.bats if skopeo can't create signatures
body:
This does not happen in this repo's tests, but containers/image's `make test-skopeo` fails in the `containers_image_openpgp` configuration with

```
not ok 10 signing
...
# time="2019-06-11T20:59:32Z" level=fatal msg="Signing not supported: signing is not supported in github.com/containers/image built with the containers_image_openpgp build tag"
```

To reproduce/test this:
```sh
make test-system BUILDTAGS='ostree containers_image_openpgp'
```

Cc: @edsantiago

comments.body:
Nice catch. Is there any other way to catch this situation earlier? `skopeo --version | grep something`? I think it'd be much nicer if we could do:
```
    if SOME CONDITION WE CAN TEST FOR; then
        skip "this skopeo doesn't support signing"
    fi
```
Yeah. `skopeo standalone-sign` with two `/dev/null`s and the like is not great, but it _is_ better than aborting the loop in the middle. Thanks for kicking me in the right direction, PTAL.

Much nicer, thank you! My one minor suggestion might be to be more flexible in the error message you check for:
```
- if [[ "$output" =~ 'signing is not supported in github.com/containers/image built with the ...
+ if [[ "$output" =~ 'signing is not supported` ]]; then
```
This allows the `containers/image` message to change (slightly) without impacting us; it also allows other TBD conditions to use 'signing is not supported'. But I'll accept this too.
